geo_constraints: Based in Vancouver, BC, Canada. ACCEPTABLE — in-person or hybrid in Greater Vancouver (Burnaby, Richmond, North Vancouver, Surrey count); fully remote roles UNAMBIGUOUSLY open to Canadian residents — explicit phrases like 'remote-Americas', 'remote-global', 'remote-worldwide', 'remote (Canada)', 'PST/Pacific time zone', or country lists that include Canada. NOT ACCEPTABLE — score 1-2 EVEN IF the role and tech stack are otherwise strong matches: (a) US-only remote ('US-based only', 'must reside in the United States', 'remote (US)', 'EST/CST/PST in the US'); (b) EU-only remote ('EU residents only', 'CET timezone', country lists like 'Bulgaria, Poland, Romania, Serbia, Ukraine'); (c) any country-specific remote that omits Canada ('remote India only', 'remote LATAM', 'remote Brazil', 'remote-Europe'); (d) JD written in a non-English language — German, French, Spanish, etc. — signals geo-restriction to that market; (e) in-person/hybrid in any non-Vancouver office: Toronto, Montreal, NYC, SF, Seattle, Austin, London, Berlin, Paris, Copenhagen, Amsterdam. DEFAULT SKEPTICAL: when the JD doesn't explicitly mention Canada, Americas, or global eligibility, score 2-3, not 4. The bar for 4-5 is: tech-stack match + seniority match + EXPLICIT eligibility for Canada-based candidates.
